---
title: "World Layoffs 2023-25"
author: "Kaustubh Sardesai"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  word_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(DBI)
library(RMySQL)

con <- dbConnect(
  RMySQL::MySQL(),
  dbname = "World_layoffs",
  host = "localhost",
  user = "root",
  password = "Majorbatra@5"
)
```

```{sql connection=con}
## Data Cleaning and Exploratory Data Analysis Project
## World Layoffs 

SELECT* 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs;
```

```{sql connection=con}
##1. Remove Duplicates 
##2. Standardise the Data 
##3. Null Values or Blank Values 
##4. Remove Any Columns


## Creating a staging table to aboid any manipulations of raw data 
```

```{sql connection=con}
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS layoffs_staging;
```

```{sql connection=con}
CREATE TABLE layoffs_staging
LIKE layoffs;
```

```{sql connection=con}
INSERT  layoffs_staging 
SELECT* 
FROM layoffs
; 
```

```{sql connection=con}
## 1. Remove Duplicates 

SELECT *,
ROW_NUMBER() OVER
(PARTITION BY company,location,total_laid_off,`date`,percentage_laid_off,industry,`source`,stage,funds_raised,country,date_added)
AS ROW_NUM   
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging
; 
##If ROW_NUM is 2 its a duplicate 
```

```{sql connection=con}
WITH duplicate_cte AS 
(
SELECT *,
ROW_NUMBER() OVER
(PARTITION BY company,location,total_laid_off,`date`,percentage_laid_off,industry,`source`,stage,funds_raised,country,date_added)
AS ROW_NUM   
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging
)
SELECT*
FROM duplicate_cte 
WHERE ROW_NUM > 1
;  
## Filtering for the duplicates using CTE 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT* 
FROM layoffs_staging
WHERE company = 'Cars24' 
; 
## Confirming the results of above qury 

```

```{sql connection=con}
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS layoffs_staging;
```

```{sql connection=con}
CREATE TABLE `layoffs_staging`(
  `company` text,
  `location` text,
  `total_laid_off` INT,
  `date` text,
  `percentage_laid_off` DECIMAL(5,2),
  `industry` text,
  `source` text,
  `stage` text,
  `funds_raised` int DEFAULT NULL,
  `country` text,
  `date_added` text,
  `ROW_NUM` INT 
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci
; 
## Created a layoffs _staging2 table to manupulate dupliate data 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT* 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
; 
```

```{sql connection=con}
INSERT INTO layoffs_staging2 (
    company,
    location,
    total_laid_off,
    `date`,
    percentage_laid_off,
    industry,
    source,
    stage,
    funds_raised,
    country,
    date_added,
    ROW_NUM
)
SELECT
    company,
    location,
    total_laid_off,
    `date`,
    percentage_laid_off,
    industry,
    source,
    stage,
    funds_raised,
    country,
    date_added,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY
            company,
            location,
            total_laid_off,
            `date`,
            percentage_laid_off,
            industry,
            source,
            stage,
            funds_raised,
            country,
            date_added
    ) AS ROW_NUM
FROM layoffs_staging;
## Added all the data from layoffs_staging table plus the ROW_NUM coloumn which is to be filtered next 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT* 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
WHERE ROW_NUM > 1
; 
## Filtered Duplicates in layoffs_staging2 table 
```

```{sql connection=con}
DELETE 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
WHERE ROW_NUM > 1
; 
##Deleted duplicates from layoffs_staging2 table 

```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT* 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2;

## Stage 1 - removing duplicates complete 
```

```{sql connection=con}
## 2. Standardise the Data 

SELECT company, (TRIM(company)) 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
;

```

```{sql connection=con}
UPDATE World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
SET company = TRIM(company)
;
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT DISTINCT industry
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
ORDER BY 1
; 
## Just to check 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT DISTINCT location
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
ORDER BY 1
; 
## Just to check
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT DISTINCT country 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
ORDER BY 1
; 
## Just to check 
```

```{sql connection=con}
UPDATE World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
SET `date` = STR_TO_DATE(`date`, '%m/%d/%Y')
WHERE `date` LIKE '%/%/%'
; 
## Converting date coloumn from text to int 
```

```{sql connection=con}
ALTER TABLE World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
MODIFY COLUMN `date` DATE
; 
## Modifying the coloumn and table 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT*
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
WHERE industry IS NULL
OR industry = ''
; 
## Found another duplicate company named appsmith - it wasnt filtered earlier as industry coloumn had no data on these rows
```

```{sql connection=con}
## Addition to stage 1 - removing the duplicates 
SELECT*
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
WHERE company = 'appsmith'
;
## making sure if there is more data on Company 'Appsmith' - only 2 lines both same 

```

```{sql connection=con}
DELETE 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
WHERE company = 'appsmith'
; 
##Deleted all layoff data from company 'Applesmith' as data couldnt be varified as accurate 

```

```{sql connection=con}
## 3. Null Values or Blank Values 
## No NULLS in relevent coloumns 

## 4. Remove Any Columns
## No Coloumns to be removed at this stage

```

```{sql connection=con}
## Exploratory Data Analysis 

SELECT* 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT MAX(total_laid_off), MAX(percentage_laid_off) 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
; 
## finding MAX 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT* 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
WHERE percentage_laid_off = 1
ORDER BY total_laid_off DESC
; 
## companies which went bust 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT* 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
WHERE percentage_laid_off = 1
ORDER BY funds_raised DESC
; 
## layoffs based on funds raised by companies 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT MIN(`date`), MAX(`date`) 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2
;
## finding start and end date of the records in the dataset 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT company, SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
GROUP BY company 
ORDER BY 2 DESC
; 
## Layoffs based on company
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT industry, SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
GROUP BY industry 
ORDER BY 2 DESC
; 
## layoffs based on industry 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT country, SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
GROUP BY country 
ORDER BY 2 DESC
;
## layoffs based on country 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT YEAR (`date`), SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
GROUP BY YEAR (`date`)
ORDER BY 1 DESC
;
## layoffs based on year 

```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT stage, SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
GROUP BY stage 
ORDER BY 2 DESC
; 
## layoffs based on fundraising stage of the company 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT substring(`date`,1,7) as `MONTH`, SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
WHERE substring(`date`,1,7) IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY `MONTH`
ORDER BY 1 ASC 
; 
## layoffs based on month of the year 
```

```{sql connection=con}
WITH rolling_total AS 
(
SELECT substring(`date`,1,7) as `MONTH`, SUM(total_laid_off) as total_fired 
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
WHERE substring(`date`,1,7) IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY `MONTH`
ORDER BY 1 ASC 
)
SELECT `MONTH`, total_fired, SUM(total_fired) OVER (ORDER BY `MONTH`) AS rolling_total 
FROM rolling_total 
; 
##rolling total of layoffs 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT company, YEAR (`date`),  SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
GROUP BY company, YEAR (`date`)
ORDER BY company ASC
; 
## layoffs per company per year 
```

```{sql connection=con}
WITH company_year (company, years, total_laid_off) AS 
(
SELECT company, YEAR (`date`),  SUM(total_laid_off)
FROM World_layoffs.layoffs_staging2 
GROUP BY company, YEAR (`date`)
),
Company_Year_Rank AS 
(
SELECT*, DENSE_RANK () OVER(PARTITION BY years ORDER BY total_laid_off DESC) AS ranking
FROM company_year
WHERE years IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY ranking ASC
)
SELECT *
FROM Company_Year_Rank 
WHERE ranking  <= 5
;
## 2 CTEs used to find out top 5 companies (in terms of employee lay offs) every year. 
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT
    DATE_FORMAT(`date`, '%Y-%m') AS month,
    SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
FROM layoffs_staging2
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month
; 
## Time based Trends

```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%201.png)

```{sql connection=con}
WITH monthly AS (
    SELECT
        DATE_FORMAT(`date`, '%Y-%m') AS month,
        SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
    FROM layoffs_staging2
    GROUP BY month
)
SELECT
    month,
    total_laid_off,
    SUM(total_laid_off) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS cumulative_layoffs
FROM monthly
;
## Rolling (Cumulative) Layoffs
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%202.png)

```{sql connection=con}
WITH monthly AS (
    SELECT
        DATE_FORMAT(`date`, '%Y-%m') AS month,
        SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
    FROM layoffs_staging2
    GROUP BY month
)
SELECT
    month,
    total_laid_off,
    SUM(total_laid_off) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS cumulative_layoffs
FROM monthly
;
## Rolling (Cumulative) Layoffs
```

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT
    company,
    SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
FROM layoffs_staging2
GROUP BY company
ORDER BY total_laid_off DESC
LIMIT 10
; 
## Top 10 Companies by Total Layoffs
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%203.png)

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT
    industry,
    SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
FROM layoffs_staging2
GROUP BY industry
ORDER BY total_laid_off DESC
;
## Layoffs by Industry
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%204.png)

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT
    country,
    SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
FROM layoffs_staging2
GROUP BY country
; 
##Layoffs by Country (Map)
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%205.png)

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT
    stage,
    SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
FROM layoffs_staging2
GROUP BY stage
ORDER BY total_laid_off DESC
;
## Layoffs by Company Stage
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%208.png)

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT
    company,
    SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off,
    MAX(funds_raised) AS funds_raised
FROM layoffs_staging2
WHERE funds_raised IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY company
; 
## Funds Raised vs Layoffs
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%206.png)

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%207.png)

```{sql connection=con}
SELECT
    company,
    industry,
    country,
    total_laid_off,
    funds_raised
FROM layoffs_staging2
WHERE percentage_laid_off = 1
ORDER BY total_laid_off DESC
;
## Companies That Shut Down (100% Layoffs)
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Sheet%209.png)

```{sql connection=con}
WITH company_year AS (
    SELECT
        company,
        YEAR(`date`) AS year,
        SUM(total_laid_off) AS total_laid_off
    FROM layoffs_staging2
    GROUP BY company, year
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT *,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY total_laid_off DESC) AS rank_
    FROM company_year
)
SELECT *
FROM ranked
WHERE rank_ <= 5
; 
## Top 5 Companies by Layoffs Each Year
```

![](Data%20Viz%20PNGs/Dashboard%201.png)
